elements.label: Rain Garden
elements.description: Shallow depression planted with native plants that captures and filters stormwater runoff
elements.mulch: 3" hardwood mulch on slopes, 2" in basin (NOT rubber mulch)
elements.overflow: Connect overflow to main drainage system or install weir at 6" depth

plants.name: Blue Flag Iris
plants.notes: Excellent rain garden plant, beautiful blue flowers

plants.name: Joe-Pye Weed
plants.notes: Large purple flower clusters, excellent for pollinators

plants.name: Coneflower
plants.notes: Purple daisy flowers, drought tolerant once established

plants.name: Daylily
plants.notes: Reliable, many color options, good for slopes

plants.name: Lavender
plants.notes: Fragrant, drought tolerant, needs excellent drainage

plants.name: Sedum
plants.notes: Succulent groundcover, excellent for dry slopes

middle_moist.description: Transition zone — moist but not flooded

elements.installation_summary: Excavate 305 sq ft area to 9" deep with gradual side slopes (3:1). Amend soil, install plants in zones (wet center, moist middle, dry edges). Mulch and connect overflow.

elements.label: Dry Creek Bed
elements.description: Natural-looking stone channel that conveys water during rain and serves as landscape feature when dry

plants.name: Coneflower
plants.notes: Purple daisy flowers, drought tolerant once established

plants.name: Daylily
plants.notes: Reliable, many color options, good for slopes

plants.name: Creeping Red Fescue
plants.notes: Fine-textured lawn grass that handles slopes well

design_tips: Curve the creek bed naturally — avoid straight lines
Vary stone sizes from large boulders to small pebbles
Place largest boulders at outside of curves
Cluster plants at bends and edges
Let some stones overlap the edges for natural look
Ensure positive grade throughout — no flat spots
Start and end at logical points (downspout, garden, property edge)
elements.installation_summary: Excavate winding channel 30ft × 5ft × 6". Line with landscape fabric, place boulders at bends, fill with river rock, plant edges.

elements.label: Planted Bioswale
elements.description: Vegetated channel that slows, filters, and infiltrates stormwater while adding beauty

specifications.check_dams: Install small stone check dams every 15-20 ft on steeper grades

plants.name: Switchgrass
plants.notes: Very adaptable native grass, excellent erosion control

plants.name: Soft Rush
plants.notes: Tolerates standing water, good for biofiltering

plants.name: Blue Flag Iris
plants.notes: Excellent rain garden plant, beautiful blue flowers

elements.installation_summary: Grade broad, shallow swale with 3:1 side slopes. Amend bottom 6" with compost. Plant dense native grasses and perennials. Install check dams on slopes >2%.

elements.label: Permeable Hardscape Recommendations
elements.description: Replace or supplement impervious surfaces to reduce runoff at the source

options.name: Permeable Pavers
options.maintenance: Vacuum or pressure wash joints annually to maintain infiltration

options.name: Grass Pavers (Turf Block)
options.application: Overflow parking, fire lanes, light-traffic areas
options.maintenance: Mow and water like regular lawn

elements.installation_summary: Evaluate existing impervious surfaces for conversion opportunities. Permeable pavers over French drain lines provide dual benefit.

principles: Manage water at the source — reduce, slow, spread, and infiltrate before conveying
Mimic natural hydrology — use plants and soil to filter and absorb runoff
Create multi-functional landscapes — every element serves drainage AND aesthetic purpose
Use native plants — adapted to local rainfall patterns, support ecosystem, low maintenance
Design for the worst, enjoy the best — system handles storms, looks great every day
design_philosophy.approach: Green Infrastructure First — use natural systems where possible, engineered systems where necessary

monthly: Inspect catch basin grates — clear debris
Check pop-up emitters — ensure they operate freely
Verify no erosion at outlet points
quarterly: Inspect French drain cleanouts — flush if needed
Check rain garden — remove sediment buildup if >1"
Inspect dry creek bed — reposition displaced stones
Check all grades near foundation — correct any settlement
annually: Flush entire pipe system with garden hose
Replenish mulch in rain garden and planting beds (spring)
Divide and replant rain garden perennials as needed
Reseed any bare spots on slopes or swales
Inspect and repair any erosion damage
after_major_storms: Walk property and check for new ponding areas
Verify all catch basins are draining
Clear any debris from grates and channels
Note any areas where system was overwhelmed — may need upgrades

topography.message: No elevation data available. Add survey points with elevations to enable topographic analysis.

soil.description: Soil type has not been determined. Analysis uses conservative assumptions. A soil test is strongly recommended.
drainage_implications: GET A SOIL TEST — results will significantly improve drainage plan accuracy
Plan may be over-designed — soil test could reveal simpler solutions
Consider a percolation test: dig 12" hole, fill with water, measure drain time
amendment_recommendations: Perform soil test: USDA Web Soil Survey or local extension office
DIY perc test: fill 12" deep hole, time to drain. >4hrs = clay, <30min = sand
soil.variation_note: Uniform soil type assumed across site
soil.perc_test_recommendation: STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Perform percolation test at 3+ locations across property

notes: Costs are estimates based on national averages — local pricing may vary
Equipment includes mini excavator rental if needed
Permit fees not included — check local requirements
DIY savings: labor portion can be reduced 50-100%

steps.title: Call 811 — Locate Underground Utilities
steps.description: Contact your local 811 service at least 48 hours before digging. Mark all utility locations. This is REQUIRED BY LAW.

steps.title: Obtain Permits
steps.description: Check with local building department for grading/drainage permits. Some jurisdictions require permits for any work affecting stormwater flow.

steps.title: Mark Layout & Establish Grade Stakes
steps.description: Use string lines and stakes to mark all trench locations, catch basin positions, and outlet points. Verify slopes with a laser level or transit.

steps.title: Strip & Stockpile Topsoil
steps.description: Remove topsoil from trench areas and stockpile for backfill. Keep separate from subsoil.

steps.title: Install Catch Basins & Set Elevations
steps.description: Set catch basins on compacted gravel base. Verify rim elevation flush with planned finished grade. Connect outlet pipes.

steps.title: Install French Drain System
steps.description: Line trenches with fabric, add gravel bed, place perforated pipe (holes DOWN), backfill with gravel, wrap fabric over top.

steps.title: Install Collector Pipes & Connect System
steps.description: Lay solid collector pipe from catch basins and French drain outlets to system outlet. Maintain minimum 1% slope throughout.

steps.title: Install Downspout Extensions
steps.description: Connect underground extensions to each downspout. Route to pop-up emitters or tie into collector system.

steps.title: Install System Outlet
steps.description: Install outlet at lowest property point. Add riprap apron for erosion protection. Verify discharge direction.

steps.title: Rough Grading
steps.description: Grade all disturbed areas to establish proper drainage slopes. Minimum 5% away from structures, 2% across yard areas.

steps.title: Fine Grading & Swale Construction
steps.description: Shape swales and fine-grade all surfaces. Compact fill. Verify all grades with level.

steps.title: Install Rain Garden
steps.description: Excavate 305 sq ft area to 9" deep with gradual side slopes (3:1). Amend soil, install plants in zones (wet center, moist middle, dry edges). Mulch and connect overflow.

steps.title: Install Dry Creek Bed
steps.description: Excavate winding channel 30ft × 5ft × 6". Line with landscape fabric, place boulders at bends, fill with river rock, plant edges.

steps.title: Install Planted Bioswale
steps.description: Grade broad, shallow swale with 3:1 side slopes. Amend bottom 6" with compost. Plant dense native grasses and perennials. Install check dams on slopes >2%.

steps.title: Install Permeable Hardscape Recommendations
steps.description: Evaluate existing impervious surfaces for conversion opportunities. Permeable pavers over French drain lines provide dual benefit.

steps.title: Seed/Sod & Mulch
steps.description: Apply seed or sod to all disturbed areas. Mulch planting beds. Install erosion control blanket on slopes.

steps.title: System Flow Test
steps.description: Run water through each system component using a garden hose. Verify flow at all catch basins, French drains, and outlet. Check for leaks or backups.

steps.title: Grade Verification
steps.description: After first rain event, walk property and verify no ponding near structures. Check all grades and make adjustments as needed.
